task_completion.description: An armature object is created in the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Outliner panel, an object with an armature icon (a stick figure) is visible.

task_completion.description: Four bones are created and correctly named.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Outliner, under the armature object, there are four bone icons named exactly 'Base', 'LowerArm', 'UpperArm', and 'Head'.

task_completion.description: The bones are parented in the correct hierarchy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Outliner, the bones are nested to show the hierarchy: 'Base' is the top-level bone, 'LowerArm' is nested under 'Base', 'UpperArm' is nested under 'LowerArm', and 'Head' is nested under 'UpperArm'.

task_completion.description: The bones are positioned to follow the lamp's structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D Viewport, the bones are visually aligned with the corresponding parts of the lamp mesh (base, lower arm, upper arm, and head).

task_completion.description: The mesh objects are parented to the armature.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Outliner, the four mesh objects ('LampBase', 'LampLowerArm', 'LampUpperArm', 'LampHead') appear nested under the Armature object, indicating they are children of the armature.

task_completion.description: The project is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title bar displays the file path ending in '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/lamp_rigged.blend', or a save dialog shows this exact path and filename.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender's 3D Viewport, Outliner, and Properties panels rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Blender application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender interface is clearly visible in the screenshots, not a text editor or a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script to bypass manual GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen pasting and executing a complete Python script in Blender's Text Editor or Python Console to instantly solve the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating an armature with exactly four bones named 'Base', 'LowerArm', 'UpperArm', and 'Head'. The bone hierarchy must be: 'Base' (root) -> 'LowerArm' -> 'UpperArm' -> 'Head'. The four mesh objects ('LampBase', 'LampLowerArm', 'LampUpperArm', 'LampHead') must be parented to the armature. The final project must be saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/lamp_rigged.blend'.
